compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: META EVIDENCE from the single-pass G3/G4 review of THIS epic (run as the merge+pack extreme — one subagent over all 6 children instead of the 2N fan-out): evaluating all children TOGETHER IMPROVED cross-child (G4) recall — it surfaced an inter-child gap (S2's unverified prompt relocation) that a one-child-at-a-time fan-out would have MISSED (each child reads fine alone; the gap lives between S2 and S6). This validates the design hypothesis that G3/G4 (cross-child criteria) benefit from seeing children together. CAVEAT: attention skewed to the first/most-annotated children; TAIL children got less granular review (recency/salience bias). => batching preserves/improves cross-child fidelity but risks per-child DEPTH on tail children; the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4. S6's eval should specifically check TAIL-child recall.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Checked the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child (S5 bin-packing) against the whole sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S6 da34) and the parent. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations:
- Implicit shared state: the `sizing.py` container-cost projection (`len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT_USD`, sizing.py:206) is touched by THREE siblings in sequence — S3 (fix base cost model + "container never shed"), S4 (2N->N), S5 (this child: <N after packing). This is real shared state, but it is EXPLICITLY ordering-resolved by the parent's Sequencing note ("Land S3 -> S4 -> S5 STRICTLY in DAG order: S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's 'container never shed' + cost-model fix lands") and the DAG (S3->S4->S5). Each landing layers a correct projection on the prior. No contradiction.
- Conflicting assumptions: S5's "Why" ("After merge, the container still makes one call per child") correctly assumes S4 has landed — matches the DAG. The ca03 "each child WHOLE / never chunked" window-fit invariant is upheld consistently with the parent's Out-of-scope (chunking forbidden) and S4's same invariant. No conflict.
- Dependency gap: S5's incoming deps are correctly modeled as `blocks` links on the blockers — S4 (98c6) blocks 1762 and S6 (da34) blocks 1762 — matching the parent DAG `S4->S5` and `S6->{S4,S5}`. S5's fidelity gate is the S6 semantic harness (DEPENDS ON S6), correctly declared. No gap.
- Scope overlap: S4 and S5 both edit `plan_review_container.md` (prompt) and `sizing.py` (projection). Because S4->S5 is strict, these are SERIAL layered edits (S4 adds the dual-rubric/attribution; S5 adds multi-child sections + required per-child output; S5 takes the sizing projection from N to <N), not concurrent contradictory ownership. The strict-ordering Sequencing note governs the sizing.py seam. Benign under ANTI-FP.
- Ordering violation: none — S5 is the terminal node of the strict S3->S4->S5 chain and is the last to touch the shared projection.
- Consumer impact: S5's bin-packer reshapes the `_run_container` call structure produced by S3's new pool + warm-then-fan-out, consuming (not contradicting) S3's "container criteria NEVER shed" guarantee. Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. Consistent.
- Residual references: S5 preserves the EXISTING single-child fallback and the "too-big -> reduce the ticket" failure finding as unchanged behavior — consistent with the parent's ca03 window-fit guarantee; no sibling carries a stale contract against a design S5 removes.

Cross-child note (not a finding; consistency-positive): the S6/S4/S5 shared comment flags that the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail "already in S5" must ALSO apply to S4 and that S6's eval should check TAIL-child recall — this is an S4/S6 obligation, not an S5 gap; S5 already carries the guardrail and the eval-gate dependency. No G4 inconsistency for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Independent re-walk of the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child (S5 bin-packing) against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S6 da34) and the parent. NO G4 FINDINGS — clean pairing.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state: `sizing.py` container-cost projection is shared by S3 (cost-model fix / 2N base + 'container never shed'), S4 (2N->N) and S5 (<N after packing). Real shared state, but EXPLICITLY ordering-resolved by the parent Sequencing note ('Land S3 -> S4 -> S5 STRICTLY in DAG order: S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's container-never-shed + cost-model fix lands') and the DAG S3->S4->S5. Each landing layers a correct projection on the prior. No contradiction.
- Conflicting assumptions: S5's Why ('the container still makes one call per child') correctly presumes S4 has landed — matches DAG S4->S5. The ca03 'each child WHOLE / never chunked' invariant is held consistently with the parent Out-of-scope (chunking forbidden) and S4. No conflict.
- Dependency gap: S5's incoming deps modeled as `blocks` on the blockers — S4 (98c6) blocks 1762 and S6 (da34) blocks 1762 — matching parent DAG S4->S5 and S6->{S4,S5}. Fidelity gate = S6 semantic harness (DEPENDS ON S6), declared. No gap.
- Scope overlap: S4 and S5 both edit `plan_review_container.md` and `sizing.py`. Strict S4->S5 ordering makes these SERIAL layered edits (S4 adds dual-rubric/attribution + 2N->N projection; S5 adds multi-child sections + required per-child output + <N projection), not concurrent contradictory ownership. The Sequencing note governs the sizing.py seam. Benign under ANTI-FP.
- Ordering violation: none — S5 is the terminal node of the strict S3->S4->S5 chain and the last to touch the shared projection.
- Consumer impact: S5's bin-packer reshapes the per-pairing call unit produced by S3's new in-`_run_container` pool + warm-then-fan-out into bins; it CONSUMES (does not contradict) S3's 'container criteria NEVER shed' guarantee and warm-first invariant. Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. Consistent.
- Residual references: S5 preserves the EXISTING single-child fallback and the 'too-big -> reduce the ticket' failure finding as unchanged — consistent with the parent ca03 window-fit guarantee; no sibling holds a stale contract against a design S5 removes.

Cross-child note (NOT a finding for this pairing): the shared S4/S5/S6 comment flags that the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail 'already in S5' must ALSO apply to S4, and that S6's eval should check TAIL-child recall. That is an S4/S6 obligation — S5 already carries the guardrail (its AC requires per-child output) and the S6 eval-gate dependency. So no G4 inconsistency attaches to (parent + S5).

Corroborates the prior container-review comment (1782577558647768001): NO G4 findings.

comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Implemented + subagent code review (remediated prefix-collision attribution) + full suite green; gate returns unsigned INDETERMINATE doc-nits.".

compiled_state.description:
# Child bin-packing for container criteria (pack small children, preserve single-child fallback)

## Why
After merge, the container still makes one call per child. Many children are small — multiple WHOLE children fit
in one call. Packing them reduces calls further, and because G3/G4 are CROSS-CHILD criteria, seeing children
together can IMPROVE consistency detection. Large (parent+child) pairings must keep the single-child path
(ca03 window-fit guarantee).

## What
- Bin-pack whole children into one call up to a token budget = model-window x P8_HEADROOM - output reserve
  (reuse the existing `pair_tokens`/`budget` + P8 machinery). Each child stays WHOLE (never chunked — ca03).
- PRESERVE the single-child path when parent+child strains the window; a child that alone exceeds budget stays
  the existing "too-big -> reduce the ticket" failure finding.
- Structured per-child sections in the prompt + REQUIRED per-child output so per-child attention isn't lost.
- Gate behind an OFFLINE EVAL confirming no per-child attention/accuracy loss vs one-child-per-call.

## Scope
- In: the bin-packer; multi-child container prompt + required per-child output; single-child fallback; the eval.
- Out: merge (S4, dependency); the caching/parallel substrate (S1/S3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Small children are packed into one call up to the window budget; each child whole (never chunked).
- [ ] A child whose (parent+child) strains the window keeps its own call; a single child exceeding budget yields
      the too-big failure finding.
- [ ] The prompt requires per-child output; an offline eval shows no per-child attention/accuracy loss vs one-per-call.
- [ ] Container call count drops below N for epics with small children; `make lint` + tests pass.

## Remediation (opus review — MAJOR 5, MINOR 10, BLOCKER 3)
- Bin budget reuses `int(largest_window_tokens * P8_HEADROOM) - P8_OUTPUT_RESERVE_TOKENS` (pass1.py:57-59), summed
  over `parent + ALL packed children`; the single-child fallback (`pair_tokens > budget`) still fires for a lone
  oversized child -> the existing too-big failure finding, unchanged.
- Update the sizing.py container-cost projection for <N calls after packing.
- Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6).
- [ ] Bin budget = parent + all packed children <= P8 window budget; a lone child exceeding it yields the existing
      too-big failure finding; each child whole (never chunked).
- [ ] sizing.py projection reflects the reduced (<N) call count after packing.
- [ ] The S6 fidelity harness shows packed-call findings within tolerance of one-child-per-call.

## CI/eval posture (S6)
- The S6 semantic fidelity eval is a HUMAN-REVIEWED development gate run before merge (live, opt-in), NOT a required/blocking CI check (non-deterministic). The offline eval-spec discipline check still runs on every PR.

file_impact.reason: child bin-packer + single-child fallback + P8 budget

compiled_state.title: Child bin-packing for container criteria (pack small children, preserve single-child fallback)
